name: cpig-render-07-synthetic-atlas
description: Phase B4 / hypothesis H3 (2026-06-18) decisive A/B vs cpig-render-06-synthetic-grid. Same synthetic 64^3 sphere SDF (radius 8 in a [-12,12]^3 box, double-signed verified), but packed into the per-atom companion atlas (u_atlasGrid, unit 4 → gridSampleAtlas) instead of the shared host grid (u_sdfGrid, unit 3 → gridSample). The conduit's atlasBaked path is what penumbra-glsl-00-sphere uses for the working tape-brick render, so this path is presumed proven. If THIS renders a blue ball but cpig-render-06 renders blank, the bug is specifically in u_sdfGrid (gridSample) — flip the dense-data routing onto gridSampleAtlas universally. If BOTH blank, the conduit's dense rendering is broken regardless of sampler unit (deeper bug). Single-PNG decisive.

setup.vlmDescription: A 3D viewport with a SMOOTH, SOLID, SHADED ball/sphere (a ray-marched SDF surface, typically tinted blue or purple) near the world origin. The defining feature is a continuous closed spherical SURFACE, filled and lit, NOT just an outline. It must NOT be an empty grey viewport, NOT just a wireframe box outline, NOT a gyroid lattice, NOT a faceted polygon mesh.

checkpoints.name: cpig-synthetic-atlas-render
checkpoints.description: Decisive H3: a synthetic 64^3 sphere SDF packed into the per-atom atlas should render via the same path tape sphere uses (gridSampleAtlas). If renders, dense-data routing should flip from u_sdfGrid → u_atlasGrid universally.
